items.title: Hollinger POTD Earl Boykins. Tiny Eastern Michigan PG was undrafted but bounced around NBA starting in 99 w/NJ, ORL, CLE & LAC. Finally broke thru as scoring bench PG for GS in 02. Signed w/DEN & he had best yrs (13 ppg). Declined after 07 but hung around til 2012. Fun player.

items.title: Hollander POTD Corey Gaines. Loyola Marymount star PG was drafted 65th by SEA in 88. Spent a decade bouncing between NBA & CBA. Best run was w/DEN & Westhead in 90-91 (8.3 ppg). Played in NBA thru 95 & minors/abroad thru 03. Was an NBA asst coach for years. Now coaches in Japan.

items.title: [Bontemps] The Nuggets have never been big spenders; the belief around the NBA is Cam Johnson (1y/$23m) or Christian Braun (5y/$125m) is likely moved to give Watson something similar per-year. "They could easily just pay him and pay the tax, but we know how the Kroenkes operate" a West scout said.
items.author: JoeBiden2020FTW
items.body_excerpt: Watson is one of the more intriguing free agents this summer, even though Denver can match any offer sheet. The 23-year-old fits one of the most coveted archetypes in the modern NBA -- versatile 6-foot-8 wing -- but retaining Watson and keeping the current roster intact would…

items.title: Hollinger POTD Raef LaFrentz. Kansas C was drafted 3rd in 98 by DEN. Good shotblocker & hit 3s, peaked at 13.8 ppg as rookie. Not strong & was bullied a bit. Traded to DAL in 02 as C of future but dumped to BOS quickly. Knee injuries slowed him down. Retired in 08 at age-31.

items.title: The Nuggets collapsed. They weren’t tough enough. That’s a culture issue.
items.author: Jake Shapiro
items.body_excerpt:
The Nuggets collapsed. They weren’t tough enough. That’s a culture issue.

The Avalanche collapsed. They weren’t tough enough. That’s a culture issue.

What do the teams’ culture have in common? They’re both led by the same self-appointed “chief culture officer.”

Josh Kroenke.

items.title: Per ESPN, OKC's 76 bench points broke the previous conference finals record since the 1984 16-team playoff format. The Lakers scored 69 in a 153-109 Game 5 clinching win vs. the Nuggets in 1985. They went on to beat the Celtics 4-2 in the NBA Finals.
items.author: Barry
items.body_excerpt:
Per ESPN, OKC's 76 bench points broke the previous conference finals record since the 1984 16-team playoff format. The Lakers scored 69 in a 153-109 Game 5 clinching win vs. the Nuggets in 1985. They went on to beat the Celtics 4-2 in the NBA Finals.

Lakers' boxscore, per Basketball-Reference:

items.title: The Thunder are the first team to score over 70 points off the bench in a conference finals or later game since the NBA divided into conferences over 50 years ago. The previous record was 69 by the Lakers in series clinching 153-109 win over the Nuggets 41 years ago to the day (5
items.author: Mike Lynch
items.body_excerpt: The Thunder are the first team to score over 70 points off the bench in a conference finals or later game since the NBA divided into conferences over 50 years ago. The previous record was 69 by the Lakers in series clinching 153-109 win over the Nuggets 41 years ago to the day (5/22/85)

items.title: The Spurs-Thunder Western Conference Finals matchup shows exactly how far the Nuggets have fallen behind the NBA’s new elite. After a first-round playoff exit, serious questions now surround Nikola Jokic, Jamal Murray, Aaron Gordon, and the future of the Nuggets roster. Shap & AP
